evals.prompt: Implement this Figma component in our React app: https://www.figma.com/design/AbC123/Marketing?node-id=210-540
evals.expected_output: The skill triggers, reads structured Figma values via the MCP, and produces a design-spec table before writing component code.
expectations: The skill is invoked from the figma.com node URL without the user naming it
It calls get_variable_defs for tokens and get_metadata for sizes/positions
It uses get_design_context for structure only and get_screenshot as visual reference only
It produces a design-spec table with columns: element, property, Figma exact value, repo token/class, source component
It does not measure any value from the screenshot
Bound Figma variables are mapped to repo token names rather than hardcoded literals

evals.prompt: What are the exact colors and spacing in this Figma frame, and how do they map to our design tokens? https://www.figma.com/design/AbC123/Marketing?node-id=210-540
evals.expected_output: The skill returns exact token-backed values, mapping each to the repo's tokens and flagging anything unbound or drifted.
expectations: get_variable_defs is treated as the source of truth for colors and spacing
Each value is mapped to a named repo token rather than a raw hex or px literal
A value with no bound Figma variable is flagged as a design smell, not silently hardcoded
Any Figma-to-token value mismatch is described as drift to log, not hardcoded to 'fix'
Every Figma call is scoped to the specific node id
